engineGuide.h2: Audi A8 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Audi A8 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: First-generation 3.0 TDI introduced with the D3 A8 in 2003. Established Audi’s diesel luxury credentials but gained notoriety for timing chain issues mounted at the rear of the engine.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure requiring engine-out replacement (typically 80k-120k miles)
EGR cooler leakage leading to coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCWA Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated Euro 5-compliant 3.0 TDI for the D4 A8 launch in 2010. Brought modest power increases while retaining the rear timing chain configuration that defined the generation’s replacement market.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 TDI, 3.0 TDI quattro, 3.0 TDI SE, 3.0 TDI Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (refined but still failure-prone)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDYA Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Late D4 generation 3.0 TDI with 286hp output. Represented the most powerful diesel variant before the D5 platform arrived, balancing performance with improved emissions compliance.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 TDI quattro, 3.0 TDI Sport, 3.0 TDI Ultra
commonFailures: EGR cooler and DPF issues (post-2010 diesels)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CGQB Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Diesel
entries.history: Audi’s twin-turbo 4.0 V8 diesel introduced with the D4 facelift in 2012. Delivered 320hp and 650Nm torque, placing the A8 among the most refined diesel luxury saloons on the UK market.
compatibleVariants: 4.0 TDI quattro, 4.0 TDI Sport, A8 L 4.0 TDI
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDTA Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: D5 generation 3.0 TDI with 286hp, meeting Euro 6d emissions standards. Introduced mild hybrid technology, adding a belt alternator starter for coasting and improved efficiency.
compatibleVariants: 50 TDI quattro, 50 TDI Sport, 50 TDI S Line
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues with urban driving
entries.cta: Get quotes for DAZA Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated D5 3.0 TDI producing 286hp with 600Nm torque. Featured in the 50 TDI badged variants, pairing diesel efficiency with quattro all-wheel drive as standard .
compatibleVariants: 50 TDI quattro, 50 TDI Pro Line Plus, 50 TDI Sport
entries.cta: Get quotes for DDV Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Supercharged 3.0 V6 FSI that debuted with the D4 A8 in 2010. Produced 290hp and introduced direct injection to the A8 petrol line-up, but brought carbon buildup on intake valves as a known trade-off.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 TFSI quattro, 3.0 TFSI SE, 3.0 TFSI Sport
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
Water pump and thermostat housing leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for CGWB Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Late D4 supercharged 3.0 TFSI producing 333hp with improved thermal management. Featured dual injection (port and direct) to reduce carbon buildup issues that plagued earlier direct-injection units.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 TFSI quattro, 3.0 TFSI Sport, A8 L 3.0 TFSI
entries.cta: Get quotes for CREC Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.2L Petrol
entries.history: 4.2 litre V8 FSI fitted to the D3 A8 producing 372hp (272kW) and 650Nm torque. Represented the naturally-aspirated peak before forced induction became dominant in the luxury class .
compatibleVariants: 4.2 FSI quattro, 4.2 FSI SE, A8 L 4.2 FSI
commonFailures: Excessive oil consumption from piston ring wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for BVN Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Twin-turbo 4.0 V8 debuting in the S8, producing 435hp. Featured cylinder-on-demand technology, shutting down four cylinders under light load, and an electric compressor to eliminate turbo lag.
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil screen clogging leading to turbo failure
Oil seepage from turbo feed lines
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTWA Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Higher-output version of the 4.0 twin-turbo V8 producing 460hp. Equipped in the later S8 models with improved cooling and revised ECU mapping for enhanced performance delivery.
compatibleVariants: S8, S8 quattro, S8 Plus (520hp version)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTWB Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: The iconic 6.0 litre W12 producing 450hp, representing the pinnacle of D3 A8 luxury. Essentially two narrow-angle V6 engines sharing a common crankshaft, offering Bentley-adjacent refinement in an Audi flagship .
compatibleVariants: A8 L W12, A8 L W12 quattro
entries.cta: Get quotes for BHT Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early 6.0 W12 fitted to the D2 facelift A8, producing 420hp. Represented Audi’s first production W12 engine, developed with input from Bentley engineers.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AZC Audi A8 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol PHEV
entries.history: Plug-in hybrid version of the D5 A8, combining the 3.0 TFSI V6 with an electric motor. Badged as the 60 TFSI e, offering electric-only range for urban zero-emission driving while retaining long-distance petrol capability.
compatibleVariants: 60 TFSI e quattro, A8 L 60 TFSI e

entries.title: hybrid control module failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for DGXA Audi A8 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Audi A8 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why 3.0 TDI Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Audi A8 3.0 TDI (2003-2014, CCWA, CDYA, CEJA engine codes)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine, requiring engine-out access for replacement. Tensioner guides become brittle with age and heat cycling, eventually cracking and allowing chain slack - leading to catastrophic valve and piston contact if not caught early.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Timing Chain Replacement - Replace chains/tensioners before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine-out replacement of timing chains, tensioners, guides, plus water pump and thermostat
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k mile lifespan; viable only if caught before chain jumps

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Post-failure solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of reconditioned engine with upgraded timing components, fully bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; upgraded components address original weakness

problems.recommendation: On an A8 3.0 TDI worth £4,000-£8,000, preventative chain replacement at a specialist makes sense if the car is otherwise sound and you caught the warning signs (rattle on cold start, particularly from the rear of the engine). However, once the chain has slipped or the engine has gone into catastrophic failure, replacement with a reconditioned unit fitted by a specialist is usually better value than a dealer repair - you get upgraded components and a warranty for less than the dealer's repair quote alone.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3.0 TDI (CCWA/CDYA) engine prices for your Audi A8

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves - Direct Injection TFSI Problems
problems.affectedModels: Audi A8 3.0 TFSI supercharged (2010-2017, CGWB, CREC engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection engines lack fuel washing over intake valves, allowing crankcase ventilation oil vapour to bake onto hot valve stems and ports. Carbon deposits restrict airflow, causing rough idle, cold-start misfires, hesitation, and eventually reduced power or stalling .

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Walnut Blast Decarbonisation - Preventive maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Intake manifold removed; media blasting clears valve carbon deposits; new gaskets and seals
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-40k miles between services; ideal every 60k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Engine - Severe buildup with valve damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of reconditioned 3.0 TFSI unit with cleaned/upgraded valve train
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; restored performance and fuel economy

problems.recommendation: Carbon cleaning at a specialist every 50,000-60,000 miles is the most cost-effective approach for 3.0 TFSI A8s. Walnut blasting for £400-£650 restores performance for less than the cost of a major service. Only consider full replacement if the engine has suffered valve damage from severe neglect - and at that point, a reconditioned unit with upgraded components is the smarter long-term play over dealer decarbonisation pricing.
problems.cta: Compare Audi A8 3.0 TFSI (CGWB/CREC) decarbonisation and engine replacement prices

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - High-Mileage 3.0 TDI & 4.0 TDI Units
problems.affectedModels: Audi A8 3.0 TDI (2003-2017, CCWA/CDYA/CGQB); Audi A8 4.0 TDI (2012-2017, CDTA)
problems.rootCause: Oil feed pipes become restricted by coked oil over time, starving the turbocharger bearings. Variable vane mechanisms in the turbo seize due to exhaust soot accumulation. On 4.0 TDI twin-turbo setups, failures in one turbo cascade to the other .

repairOptions.tier: Turbocharger Replacement - Single unit, before secondary damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of failed turbo unit; oil feed line cleaning/replacement; new gaskets and seals

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Engine - Catastrophic turbo failure (metal ingestion)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of reconditioned engine with upgraded turbo oiling system and new turbo(s)
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; upgraded oiling components prevent recurrence

problems.recommendation: If you catch turbo failure early (whistling, loss of power, excessive smoke), specialist turbo replacement for £1,300-£2,000 is the right call - especially on a sub-120k mile car. However, once a turbo has disintegrated and sent metal debris through the intake or lubrication system, the engine is compromised. A full reconditioned engine with upgraded oiling components and new turbos is the only reliable path forward; repair attempts almost always fail again within months.
problems.cta: Compare Audi A8 turbo replacement and reconditioned engine prices

problems.h4: Excessive Oil Consumption - 4.2 FSI V8 Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Audi A8 4.2 FSI quattro (2006-2010, BVN engine code)
problems.rootCause: The 4.2 FSI V8 suffers from piston ring design flaws that allow excessive oil to bypass the rings and burn in the combustion chamber. Carbon buildup in the ring grooves exacerbates the issue, leading to consumption of 1 litre per 600-1,000 miles .

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Piston Ring Replacement - Engine-out rebuild
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine strip; replacement of piston rings, seals, and gaskets; cylinder honing
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k mile lifespan for repair; high labour cost

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Engine - Complete resolution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of reconditioned engine with upgraded piston rings and updated components
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; upgraded ring design prevents recurrence

problems.recommendation: On a 4.2 FSI A8 currently worth £3,000-£6,000, intermediate piston ring replacement at a specialist for £2,800-£4,200 is borderline - it often approaches the car's value. Unless the car is in exceptional condition or has sentimental value, replacement with a reconditioned engine (which includes upgraded rings and a full warranty) is usually better value than sinking dealer-level money into a partial rebuild. For high-mileage examples showing other signs of wear, consider comparing reconditioned engine quotes before committing to repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Audi A8 4.2 FSI reconditioned engines with upgraded piston rings

problems.h4: EGR Cooler & DPF Problems - Post-2010 Diesel Emissions Complexities
problems.affectedModels: Audi A8 3.0 TDI (2010-2017, CDYA/CGQB); Audi A8 4.0 TDI (2012-2017, CDTA)
problems.rootCause: EGR coolers leak coolant internally, leading to white smoke, coolant loss, and potentially hydrolock. DPF systems clog prematurely on vehicles used primarily for short urban trips where regeneration cycles cannot complete .

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cooler Replacement / DPF Cleaning - Targeted fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR cooler replacement; forced DPF regeneration or chemical cleaning; coolant flush

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Engine - Combined emissions system failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of reconditioned engine with new EGR cooler, cleaned/refurbished DPF, upgraded components

problems.recommendation: Isolated EGR cooler or DPF issues are worth repairing at a specialist for £450-£800, especially if the car is otherwise reliable and sees mixed driving patterns. However, when both systems fail simultaneously (common after 90k miles on urban-driven A8s), the combined repair cost often approaches £1,500-£2,000. At that point, a reconditioned engine with new emissions components fitted - often only slightly more expensive than the cumulative repairs - offers better value and a full warranty.
problems.cta: Compare Audi A8 diesel EGR/DPF repair and replacement engine quotes

problems.h4: Engine Misfire & Injector Failure - Fuel System Collapse
problems.affectedModels: Audi A8 3.0 TDI (2003-2014, CCWA/CDYA); Audi A8 3.0 TFSI (2010-2017, CGWB/CREC)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on TDI engines fail due to internal wear, causing misfires, rough running, and fuel dilution of engine oil. On TFSI petrol units, ignition coils fail progressively, while injector clogging from carbon deposits causes lean misfires .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of failed injectors (diesel) or coils (petrol); fuel system flush if contamination present

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Engine - Fuel system contamination/engine damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of reconditioned engine with new injectors, fuel system components, upgraded parts

problems.recommendation: On a TDI, never ignore injector failure - fuel dilution of engine oil destroys bearings rapidly. Single injector replacement at a specialist for £800-£1,500 is sensible if caught immediately. However, once multiple injectors have failed or the engine has run with fuel-diluted oil, bearing damage is likely. In that scenario, a reconditioned engine with new injectors and a complete fuel system service is the only reliable answer. On TFSI petrol units, coil replacement is cheap - but injector failure often warrants a full decarbonisation service at the same time.
problems.cta: Compare Audi A8 injector repair and reconditioned engine quotes today

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Audi A8
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing chain failure on a 3.0 TDI, carbon buildup on a TFSI petrol, or turbo failure on a high-mileage diesel, the decision between repair and replacement comes down to honest maths - and EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Audi A8 specialists. Every reconditioned engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery or supply-and-fit options. Compare prices today and get your A8 back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Audi A8 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Audi A8 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.backDescription: Reconditioned A8 engines are dismantled, inspected and rebuilt to precise factory specifications using new critical components.

engineSizes.h2: Audi A8 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Audi A8 across all generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes to help you identify your exact unit.

items.title: Audi A8 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0 V6 TDI has been the backbone of the Audi A8 diesel range across three generations - D3 (2003-2010), D4 (2010-2017) and early D5 (2017-2022). Multiple power outputs from 204hp to 286hp were offered, with CCWA, CDYA and CGQB codes dominating UK replacement demand. Rear-mounted timing chain tensioner failure remains the most discussed issue among owners, particularly on pre-2014 units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi A8 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi A8 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The supercharged 3.0 V6 TFSI arrived with the D4 A8 in 2010, producing 290hp in CGWB form and later 333hp in CREC guise. Direct injection brought excellent fuel economy for a luxury saloon but introduced carbon buildup on intake valves as a maintenance consideration. Late D4 units gained dual injection to mitigate the issue, and the engine continued into early D5 production as a 340hp mild hybrid.
commonFailurePoints: Carbon buildup on intake valves (50k-80k miles)
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A8 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 4.2 V8 FSI (code BVN) was offered in the late D3 A8 from 2006 to 2010, producing 372hp and delivering a traditional V8 character before forced induction took over. This engine is known for its responsive throttle and refined soundtrack, but high-mileage units developed a reputation for excessive oil consumption due to piston ring wear.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption from piston ring wear
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A8 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The twin-turbo 4.0 V8 TFSI launched in the D4 S8 (2012-2015) producing 435hp (CTWA), later upgraded to 460hp (CTWB) and 520hp in the S8 Plus. Cylinder-on-demand technology shuts down four cylinders under light load for efficiency, while an electric compressor eliminates turbo lag. Oil screen blockages leading to turbo failure are the most critical issue to monitor.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger oil screen clogging causing turbo failure
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A8 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary W12 - essentially two narrow-angle V6 engines sharing a common crankshaft - was fitted to the D2 (AZC, 2001-2002, 420hp) and D3 (BHT, 2004-2010, 450hp) A8 L models. This engine delivered Bentley-grade refinement and effortless torque, making the A8 a genuine luxury flagship. Replacement demand is low but very high-value when failure occurs, with timing chain service requiring an engine-out procedure.
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 6.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Audi A8 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Audi A8 3.0L Petrol Plug-In Hybrid Engines
items.description: The D5 A8 gained a plug-in hybrid variant badged 60 TFSI e, pairing a 3.0 V6 TFSI petrol engine with an electric motor for a combined 462hp. Available from 2021 onwards, this powertrain offers electric-only urban driving alongside long-distance petrol capability. High-voltage battery degradation and complex hybrid system faults are the primary specialist replacement considerations. Not sure which engine size your Audi A8 needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Audi A8 specialists.
items.cta: Enquire about Audi A8 3.0L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Audi A8 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Audi A8 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the early D3 3.0 TDI to the latest D5 60 TFSI e plug-in hybrid.

items.title: Audi A8 Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0 V6 TDI dominated A8 diesel production across three generations (D3, D4, D5), with the 4.0 V8 TDI offered as a premium torque-rich alternative.
items.descriptor: The 3.0 V6 TDI dominated A8 diesel production across three generations (D3, D4, D5), with the 4.0 V8 TDI offered as a premium torque-rich alternative.
items.familiesLabel: Common Audi A8 Diesel Engine Families
families: CCWA/CDYA - first and second-gen 3.0 TDI (204-245hp), timing chain tensioner vulnerability
CGQB/CRCA - late D4 3.0 TDI (258-286hp), turbo and EGR cooler concerns
CDTA - 4.0 V8 twin-turbo TDI (320-350hp), complex oil-fed turbo system
DAZA/DDV - D5 3.0 TDI (286hp), mild hybrid 48V system added
foundIn: D4 A8 (2010-2017) - 3.0 TDI (CDYA, CGQB, CRCA), 4.0 TDI (CDTA)
knownFor: Strong motorway economy - 40+ mpg achievable on 3.0 TDI
Timing chain tensioner failure on rear-mounted chains (80k-120k miles)
EGR cooler leaks and DPF clogging on short-trip urban cars
High UK replacement demand - 3.0 TDI accounts for over 60% of A8 engine enquiries

items.title: Audi A8 Petrol Engines
items.description: Supercharged 3.0 V6 TFSI units dominated the D4 petrol range, while the D3 offered a 4.2 V8 FSI and legendary W12, and the D5 introduced twin-turbo 4.0 V8 power.
items.descriptor: Supercharged 3.0 V6 TFSI units dominated the D4 petrol range, while the D3 offered a 4.2 V8 FSI and legendary W12, and the D5 introduced twin-turbo 4.0 V8 power.
items.familiesLabel: Common Audi A8 Petrol Engine Families
families: CGWB/CREC - supercharged 3.0 V6 TFSI (290-333hp), carbon buildup on valves
BVN - 4.2 V8 FSI (372hp), oil consumption from piston ring wear
CTWA/CTWB/CVPA - twin-turbo 4.0 V8 TFSI (435-520hp), S8 and S8 Plus
AZC/BHT - W12 (420-450hp), engine-out timing chain service
CYGA/DBZA - D5 3.0 TFSI (340hp), mild hybrid assistance
foundIn: D4 A8 (2010-2017) - 3.0 TFSI (CGWB, CREC), S8 4.0 TFSI (CTWA, CTWB)
knownFor: Supercharged 3.0 TFSI delivers seamless power with no turbo lag
Carbon buildup on intake valves requires periodic walnut blasting (50k-80k miles)
4.0 V8 S8 units suffer turbo oil screen blockages - critical failure point
W12 units are complex and costly to rebuild but exceptionally smooth

items.title: Audi A8 Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The D5 A8 gained a plug-in hybrid variant in 2021, combining the 3.0 V6 TFSI petrol engine with an electric motor for powerful yet efficient flagship motoring.
items.descriptor: The D5 A8 gained a plug-in hybrid variant in 2021, combining the 3.0 V6 TFSI petrol engine with an electric motor for powerful yet efficient flagship motoring.
families: DGXA - 3.0 TFSI plug-in hybrid (60 TFSI e), combined 462hp
Petrol engine paired with electric motor and high-voltage battery (approx 17.9 kWh usable)
Electric-only range of approximately 30-35 miles for zero-emission urban driving
Petrol engine activates automatically for longer journeys or when battery depleted
importantNotes: No pure diesel hybrid or full hybrid (non-plug-in) version of the A8 exists for the UK market
Mild hybrid (MHEV) 48V systems were added to D5 diesel models (DAZA/DDV) but are not standalone hybrids
PHEV engine replacement requires high-voltage system inspection and specialist hybrid certification
Availability through standard engine suppliers is more limited - use our network for specialist sourcing

engineYears.h2: Audi A8 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 1994 D2 launch to the current D5 generation, this timeline covers every major engine change across the Audi A8's production history, helping you identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original aluminium-bodied A8 launched with conventional V6 and V8 petrol engines - robust units that routinely reached 300,000-400,000km with proper maintenance .
keyChanges: All-aluminium Audi Space Frame (ASF) technology debuts
Quattro all-wheel drive standard on V8 models, optional on V6
No diesel options in early D2 production
knownFor: Legendary ASF aluminium construction reduces weight significantly
4.2 V8 is exceptionally robust - easily exceeds 200k miles with care
Conventional port injection avoids carbon buildup issues
Suspension wear and electrical gremlins more common than engine failure
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1996 Audi A8 replacement engines

years.preview: D2: S8 Arrives & V6 Power Boost
years.description: The original S8 launched with a 340hp 4.2 V8, while the 2.8 V6 gained power to 193hp - establishing Audi's performance luxury credentials .
keyChanges: S8 debuts with 4.2 V8 (335-340hp) and 6-speed manual option
Quattro becomes standard on all UK models
knownFor: S8 sets new benchmark for performance luxury sedans
4.2 V8 in S8 tune is highly sought-after by enthusiasts
All engines remain naturally aspirated and port-injected
Manual S8 models (rare in UK) command premium values
years.ticker: Audi A8 S8 engine replacement • Audi A8 4.2 V8 rebuild cost • D2 A8 engine reliability

years.preview: D2: W12 Flagship Arrives & Diesel Age Begins
years.description: The legendary 6.0 W12 joined the range in 2001 with 420hp, while Audi's first A8 diesels - 2.5 V6 TDI and 3.3 V8 TDI - revolutionised luxury car running costs .
keyChanges: First diesel A8s: 2.5 V6 TDI (150-180hp) and 3.3 V8 TDI (225hp)
knownFor: W12 is complex but surprisingly reliable for a 12-cylinder flagship
Early TDI units are exceptionally economical (7-9L/100km)
2.5 TDI suffers from injector pump and turbo issues at high mileage
3.3 V8 TDI is rare in UK - parts can be difficult to source
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2002 Audi A8 replacement engines
years.ticker: Audi A8 W12 engine replacement cost • Audi A8 2.5 TDI engine problems • D2 A8 diesel engine availability

years.preview: D3 Launch: FSI Direct Injection Arrives
years.description: The second-generation D3 A8 introduced FSI direct injection petrol engines (3.2 V6, 4.2 V8) - bringing efficiency gains but also carbon buildup problems for the first time .
keyChanges: New D3 platform (Typ 4E) with larger dimensions
FSI direct injection petrol engines debut
3.0 TDI diesel arrives (233hp) - becomes UK best-seller
knownFor: 3.0 TDI becomes dominant UK engine - strong economy (35+ mpg)
FSI engines suffer first carbon buildup issues (50k-80k miles)
4.2 FSI has timing chain at rear - expensive replacement
3.0 TDI timing chain tensioner issues begin to emerge

years.description: The D3 facelift brought the single-frame grille and a thunderous 5.2 V10 from the Lamborghini Gallardo to the S8 - delivering 444hp of naturally aspirated fury .
keyChanges: Single-frame trapezoidal grille adopted across range
S8 debuts with 5.2 V10 FSI (444hp) - shared with Lamborghini Gallardo
Audi Side Assist and Lane Assist debut
knownFor: V10 S8 is an enthusiast icon - Gallardo engine in a luxury saloon
4.2 TDI V8 (326hp) incredibly efficient for its size - 30+ mpg achievable
V10 maintenance is specialist-only - timing chain service requires engine-out
3.0 TDI remains the sensible, reliable choice for most buyers

years.description: The D3's final years represent the most refined and reliable iteration of this generation - with most common issues identified and addressed .
keyChanges: 4.2 TDI V8 refined for improved reliability
knownFor: Late D3 represents best value in used A8 market
3.0 TDI timing chain tensioner still a concern at 100k+ miles
4.2 TDI V8 is remarkably robust - many exceed 250k miles
FSI carbon cleaning needed every 50k-60k miles on petrols

years.description: The D4 A8 launched with supercharged 3.0 TFSI V6 petrols and - for the first time - twin-turbo 4.0 TFSI V8 power in the S8, setting new standards for efficiency and performance .
keyChanges: Supercharged 3.0 TFSI V6 petrol replaces naturally aspirated V6/V8
4.0 TFSI twin-turbo V8 debuts in S8 (520hp)
3.0 TDI continues with revised power outputs (204-250hp)

Final production year before D5 launch

60 TFSI e plug-in hybrid discontinued after 2022

faq.h2: Audi A8 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the 3.0 TDI in the Audi A8 prone to timing chain failure?
items.answer: Yes, early 3.0 TDI units (2003-2014) have rear-mounted timing chain tensioner issues, typically failing between 60k-120k miles. Late 2014-onwards units are more reliable.
keyPoints: Chain tensioner guide breaks, causing chain slack and potential valve/piston contact
Failure often occurs without warning - engine-out replacement is required
Low-mileage failures reported (some as early as 40k miles)
Pre-2014 CCWA and CDYA codes are highest risk
Preventative replacement at specialist costs £1,800-£2,800
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 3.0 TDI timing chain replacement quotes today

items.question: How much does an Audi A8 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Expect £1,350-£2,800 for a reconditioned 3.0 TDI supply-only, or £4,500-£6,500 supply and fit. Dealership replacement can exceed £9,000-£13,000 for the same work.
keyPoints: Used 3.0 TDI engines: £900-£2,500 depending on mileage and history
Rebuilt/reconditioned: £1,350-£2,800 with 12-month warranty
4.0 V8 and W12 units cost significantly more (£2,800-£5,800+)
Specialist fitting adds £600-£1,200 to engine price
One owner saved over £8,000 by choosing reconditioned over dealer new
items.cta: Get free Audi A8 engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Which Audi A8 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The late-production 3.0 TDI (CGQB, 2014-2017) is the most reliable diesel option. For petrol, the supercharged 3.0 TFSI (CREC, 2014-2017) is solid if carbon-cleaned regularly.
keyPoints: 2014-2017 3.0 TDI has most timing chain issues resolved
Late 3.0 TFSI gained dual injection to reduce carbon buildup
4.2 TDI V8 (CDTE, 385hp) is remarkably robust - many exceed 250k miles
Avoid early 4.2 FSI (pre-2010) due to oil consumption issues
What Car? recommends 50 TDI as the best all-rounder
items.cta: Compare prices for Audi A8 reconditioned engines by engine code

items.question: What causes the 4.0 TFSI V8 in the S8 to fail catastrophically?
items.answer: Oil screen blockages in turbo feed lines starve the turbos of oil, leading to complete turbo failure. Once debris enters the oil system, the engine is often beyond repair.
keyPoints: Screen clogs with coked oil between 60k-100k miles
One owner reported $53,800 (£43k+) repair cost under warranty
Failure can occur without warning - no dashboard light beforehand
Specialist replacement with upgraded oiling system is essential
Affects CTWA, CTWB, CVPA, CVPC codes in D4 and D5 S8 models
items.cta: Enquire about upgraded 4.0 TFSI reconditioned engines for your S8

items.question: Does the Audi A8 3.0 TFSI suffer from carbon buildup?
items.answer: Yes. Direct-injection 3.0 TFSI engines (CGWB 2010-2014) build carbon on intake valves every 50k-80k miles, causing rough idle, misfires and reduced power.
keyPoints: Symptoms include cold-start hesitation and loss of low-end torque
Walnut blasting at specialist costs £400-£650, dealer £800-£1,200
Late 2014-2017 CREC engine gained dual injection - less prone
D5 55 TFSI (CYGA/DBZA) mild hybrid also affected but less severe
Regular high-quality oil changes help slow buildup rate
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 3.0 TFSI carbon cleaning and engine replacement prices

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Audi A8 engine after catastrophic failure?
items.answer: Replace with a reconditioned unit. Once a 3.0 TDI has jumped timing or a 4.0 V8 has suffered turbo failure, repair costs often approach or exceed replacement - without warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure
12-month unlimited mileage warranty included with replacement
Dealer engine-out chain repair on 3.0 TDI: £3,500-£5,000 for partial fix
Reconditioned engine supply and fit: £4,500-£6,500 with full warranty
One owner paid £2,200 for a full engine rebuild rather than chasing individual failures
items.cta: Get a quote comparison for Audi A8 engine replacement vs repair

items.question: Is the Audi A8 4.2 FSI known for oil consumption problems?
items.answer: Yes. The 4.2 FSI V8 (BVN code, 2006-2010) suffers from piston ring wear, consuming 1 litre per 600-1,000 miles at higher mileages - a full rebuild is the only permanent fix.
keyPoints: Problem affects D3 generation 4.2 FSI from 2006-2010
Oil consumption typically begins around 70k-100k miles
Piston ring replacement at specialist costs £2,800-£4,200
Reconditioned engine with upgraded rings costs £4,500-£6,500
Early detection via frequent oil checks prevents secondary damage
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Audi A8 4.2 FSI engine with upgraded piston rings

items.question: How long do Audi A8 engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained 3.0 TDI diesels regularly exceed 200k-250k miles. The 4.2 TDI V8 can reach 250k+ miles. Petrol 3.0 TFSI units typically last 150k-200k miles with proper carbon cleaning.
keyPoints: 3.0 TDI with preventative timing chain work: 250k+ miles achievable
4.2 TDI V8 (CDTA/CDTE) is the most durable - documented 300k miles
3.0 TFSI lifespan limited by carbon buildup - regular cleaning extends life
EA837 petrol V6 can last 190k+ miles with proper maintenance
Average lifespan quoted at ~300,000 km (186,000 miles) for 4.0 V8
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 reconditioned engine prices with extended warranty

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a high-mileage Audi A8?
items.answer: Yes - if the car is otherwise sound. D3 and early D4 A8s with good bodywork, suspension and interior are worth £4,000-£8,000 - a £4,500-£6,500 reconditioned engine makes financial sense.
keyPoints: Factor current vehicle value against £4,500-£6,500 replacement cost
One owner spent £5,600 total on full A8 restoration including engine
Reconditioned engine includes warranty - unlike used or repair
D4 facelift (2014-2017) models hold value particularly well
Compare to new car cost - £4,500-£6,500 is 10-20% of new A8 price
items.cta: Get an Audi A8 engine replacement quote - no obligation

items.question: What are common DPF and EGR problems on Audi A8 diesels?
items.answer: 3.0 TDI diesels (especially 2010-2017) suffer EGR cooler leaks and DPF clogging from short trips. EGR replacement costs £450-£800 at specialists; DPF issues often require forced regeneration.
keyPoints: EGR cooler leaks coolant internally - white smoke and coolant loss symptoms
DPF clogs on urban-driven cars where regeneration cycles can't complete
Combined EGR/DPF failure repair approaches £1,500-£2,000
Reconditioned engine with new emissions components costs £4,200-£6,000
Regular longer journeys help DPF self-clean - avoid short trips only
items.cta: Compare Audi A8 diesel EGR/DPF repair and replacement engine quotes
